summary: Set 044 Session 2 of 6 — Claude Code log harvest + cross-backend comparative analysis. Investigation-heavy session, no code shipped. (1) Structural inventory of Claude Code 2.1.63 ~/.claude/ at discovery-notes-claude.md: 16 artifact rows in §1 inventory matrix (settings.json, .credentials.json, ide/*.lock with authToken plaintext, projects/<slug>/<conv-uuid>.jsonl + subagents/ + tool-results/, file-history/ content-addressed blob store, sessions/<pid>.json, session-env/, todos/, history.jsonl, plans/*.md, shell-snapshots/, telemetry/, cache/, plugins/, debug/, backups/); 7 distinct JSONL `type` values enumerated from a 713-line representative sample (assistant, user-prompt, user-tool-result-bearing, attachment, file-history-snapshot, ai-title, last-prompt, queue-operation); 9 distinct `toolUseResult` shapes mapped to tools (Edit/Write/Bash/Bash-older/Grep/LS/AskUserQuestion/TodoWrite/Read); Dabbler SessionStart hook integration characterized from installOrchestratorHookClaudeCode.ts + claude-session-start-invoker.js shim source (opt-in via wizard, not currently installed for this repo, hook adds no JSONL signal of its own). Reading-side discipline strictly applied: distinct-key-set enumeration / byte counts / enum-cardinality probes only against operator's REAL Claude state (31 project slugs, 121 transcripts for this repo alone) — no transcript content read into the conversation or quoted in any committed file. (2) Live-run harvest DEFERRED to S3 per operator decision mid-session 2026-05-22 ('Defer live runs to S3'). Both the S1-deferred Copilot runs AND the new S2 Claude runs fold into S3 alongside narration design, where they naturally need a baseline against which to measure narration delta. Scaffolding for the future live runs at discovery-notes-claude.md §6 (Claude-side harness layered on top of S1's scratch tree). (3) Cross-backend baseline-comparison.md authored (8 sections). Headline verdict: both backends already surface the (conv-id, file-path, tool-name, turn-index, timestamp, engine, provider) tuple a conflict watcher needs; narration design problem reduces to TWO signals (A3 + C3). §2 side-by-side table: 9 symmetric rows, 6 divergent rows all in Claude's favor (A2 per-turn message.model, A5 per-event sessionId, B1 inline args by default, B4 empirically confirmed in S2 own transcript, B5 full diff inline, C5 explicit linked event via sourceToolAssistantUUID). §3 enumerates 4 invariants + 5 divergences. §6 risk/asymmetry summary feeds S5: Claude file-history/ blob store has no Copilot equivalent — biases proposal toward read-in-place + project-to-summary, not copy-then-process; Claude has SessionStart hook surface for automatic narration, Copilot does not. (4) harvest-objectives-and-redaction.md refined: added 3 scrub rules (8 = file-history/ blob store copy-exclusion, 9 = plans/ filename slug-leak, 10 = last-prompt JSONL event-type filtering); added cross-backend copy-exclusion table listing 5 paths (Copilot+Claude IDE locks, Claude credentials, Claude file-history, Copilot OAuth credential location TBD pending S3 empirical probe); refinement window extended from 'closed after S2' to 'open through S4' to accommodate narration-emitted log surfaces from S3/S4. (5) Round A verification via gemini-pro: first call REJECTED with FOUR must-fix issues (Copilot OAuth credential missing from exclusion table; refinement-window closed prematurely; headline oversimplifies asymmetric-availability harvester-design problem; symmetry tally does not acknowledge populated-vs-empty methodological bias); all four applied in-line. Re-run VERIFIED. One nice-to-have applied post-verdict: empirical probe of assistant.message.usage + stop_details key-sets across 327-event sample revealed 10 usage fields (cache_*/inference_geo/input_tokens/iterations/output_tokens/server_tool_use/service_tier/speed) and empty stop_details — NO reasoning_effort or thinking_tokens field; service_tier+speed (both 'standard' in sample) carry the /fast-vs-standard axis; high/medium/low effort axis confirmed absent. A3 row tightened in both discovery-notes-claude.md §4 and baseline-comparison.md §2. (6) Cumulative routed Set 044 spend after S2: $0.11620 of $15.00 NTE (S5 budget allocation). §7 open empirical questions across both docs: 9 questions inherited forward to S3 live runs (including B4 Copilot live confirmation, A2 sessions.host_type empirical, A3 Copilot OTel reasoning_effort, --output-format json schema, Claude --print JSONL parity, hook full payload, session-env/ population trigger, session_refs.ref_type enumeration, telemetry activation triggers, Copilot OAuth credential location).
files_changed: docs/session-sets/044-ai-chat-log-discovery-and-experiments/harvest-objectives-and-redaction.md (refined: cross-backend exclusion table + scrub rules 8/9/10 + S4-open refinement window)

reason.specifics: Session 3 (Narration design + Copilot narration experiment) builds directly on the S2 cross-backend coverage matrix. baseline-comparison.md §5 already scopes the narration design to TWO signals (C3 mandatory; A3 conditional for the high/medium/low axis since fast/standard is empirically derivable). S3 will: (a) design the narration discipline (format + placement: structured prefix marker vs JSON-line emit, pre-tool-call vs post-tool-call, free-form vs strict) and lock the design before applying, (b) optionally route the design through cross-provider consensus (S3 budget category per spec), (c) execute the previously-DEFERRED live runs from both S1 (Copilot) and S2 (Claude) against the synthetic-set at C:\tmp\dabbler-log-harvest\synthetic-set\ to establish the baseline that the narration variant will be measured against, (d) apply narration discipline to Copilot via configuration/system-prompt and re-run, (e) quantify the delta vs the now-fresh baseline. The 9 open empirical questions in baseline-comparison.md §7 + discovery-notes-claude.md §7 (including Copilot OAuth credential location for the still-TBD row in the cross-backend exclusion table) are answerable in S3's live-run phase. Continuous Claude check-out preserves H1 single-writer lock invariant; S1 redaction policy with the S2 cross-backend exclusion table and scrub rules 8/9/10 carries forward unchanged.
